items.headline: Pogačar demolishes a legend — a record ride up Alpe d'Huez
items.body: The Tour hit its most famous mountain for the first time in four years, and Tadej Pogačar turned it into a highlight reel. Down more than three minutes to a big breakaway, he attacked at the bottom of the 21 hairpins and reeled everyone in, dropping Richard Carapaz and Lenny Martínez to win Stage 19 in the yellow jersey — and he flew up the climb in 35:27, smashing Marco Pantani's decades-old record by over two minutes. The Tour wraps Sunday in Paris.
items.say: Did you see Pogačar today? He was three minutes down on Alpe d'Huez — the famous one with all the switchbacks — and he chased everyone down AND broke a 25-year climbing record. Kind of absurd. The Tour finishes Sunday.

links.text: Cyclingnews · Pogačar flies to victory atop Alpe d'Huez

items.headline: F1's last dance before the break — the Hungarian GP this weekend
items.body: Formula 1 rolls into Budapest for the twisty, no-overtaking-anywhere Hungaroring, the final race before F1 shuts down for its three-week summer break. Practice and qualifying run through Saturday, with the race Sunday at 9am ET. All eyes are on 19-year-old championship leader Kimi Antonelli, fresh off his Spa win, trying to stretch his cushion before everyone disappears on holiday.
items.say: F1's got its last race this weekend before the summer break — the Hungarian GP. That teenager Antonelli's leading the whole championship right now. Sunday morning if you want it on. You keeping up with F1 this year?

links.text: ESPN · Hungarian GP start time & how to watch in the US

items.headline: Football's really back — veterans report and the No. 1 pick cashes in
items.body: Camps are filling up: veterans report across the league this week, and the top of the 2026 draft just got paid — No. 1 overall pick Fernando Mendoza signed with the Raiders on a fully-guaranteed four-year, $57.2 million deal. Cue the position battles, the 'best shape of his life' stories, and the first whiff of real football since the confetti fell in February.
items.say: NFL guys are all reporting to camp now — feels like football's finally back. The No. 1 overall pick just signed with the Raiders for like $57 million guaranteed too. You following your team's camp at all?

links.text: Yahoo Sports · Training camp live updates — Mendoza-Raiders deal
NFL.com · 2026 camp report dates for all 32 teams

items.headline: The rumor mill is spinning — Trout and Skubal headline deadline week
items.body: The Aug. 3 trade deadline is 10 days out and the big names are finally in play: the buzziest whisper has Angels icon Mike Trout possibly landing with the Phillies close to his hometown, while Tigers ace Tarik Skubal is the arm every contender (hi, Cubs) is dreaming about. Nothing's done yet — but the next week and a half is baseball's version of silly season.
items.say: The baseball trade deadline's next week and the rumors are getting fun — people are even floating Mike Trout to the Phillies, and everybody wants the Tigers' ace Skubal. You following any of the deadline stuff?

links.text: ESPN · 2026 trade deadline tracker: rumors & analysis

items.headline: Ankalaev headlines in Abu Dhabi — UFC Fight Night on Saturday
items.body: The fights land Saturday from Etihad Arena in Abu Dhabi, headlined by a light-heavyweight banger: top-ranked wrecking ball Magomed Ankalaev takes on the dangerous Bogdan Guskov. It's an afternoon card for US viewers (main card noon ET) streaming on Paramount+, so it's the easy 'have it on with lunch' watch this weekend.
items.say: There's UFC on Saturday — Ankalaev headlining out of Abu Dhabi, and it's actually a lunchtime card here for once. Easy one to throw on. You watch the fights?

links.text: UFC · Fight Night: Ankalaev vs. Guskov event page

items.headline: Tyla's back — 'A*POP' drops today and she's already trending worldwide
items.body: The Grammy-winning South African star behind 'Water' returns with her second album 'A*POP,' a 14-track set she calls 'popiano' — a glossy blend of pop, R&B and South African amapiano. It's stacked with the singles 'Chanel' and 'She Did It Again' (with Zara Larsson), and it shot straight to No. 1 on X in South Africa within hours of dropping. Pure summer-playlist fuel.
items.say: Tyla — the 'Water' girl — just dropped a new album today, and she's got a song with Zara Larsson on it. Perfect windows-down summer stuff. You into her?

links.text: Tyla — 'She Did It Again' ft. Zara Larsson (Official Video, TylaVEVO)

items.headline: WILLOW goes quiet and confident — 'The Thread' lands today
items.body: Willow Smith drops her ninth album 'The Thread' today, and critics are calling it her most focused, emotionally grounded work yet — a 'devotional' record of fingerpicked guitars, warm bass and hushed harmonies, made again with longtime collaborator Chris Greatti. After years of shape-shifting between pop, rock and R&B, this one just breathes. A great late-night headphones listen.
items.say: WILLOW put out a new album today — it's the calm, pretty, headphones-at-midnight kind, not the pop-punk stuff she did a few years back. Reviews are really good. You keep up with her music?

items.headline: Middle-earth returns — 'Rings of Power' Season 3 teaser drops at Comic-Con
items.body: The biggest trailer moment of Comic-Con's Friday: Prime Video used its Hall H panel to unveil the first Season 3 teaser for 'The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power' — including the first real look at Daniel Weyman's Gandalf and Sauron mid-scheme as the show barrels toward him forging the One Ring. Season 3 lands November 11. If you bailed on the show, this is the 'okay, maybe I'm back in' trailer.
items.say: They dropped the Rings of Power Season 3 teaser at Comic-Con today — you finally see Gandalf show up, and it's building to Sauron making the One Ring. Comes out in November. You ever get into that show?

links.text: Yahoo · What the Rings of Power S3 teaser reveals
TechTimes · S3 unveils Gandalf, teaser at SDCC

items.headline: Will Ferrell's golf comedy 'The Hawk' is quietly ruling Netflix
items.body: Ferrell's first-ever TV comedy is climbing the charts: he plays Lonnie 'The Hawk' Hawkins, a washed-up 2004 world No. 1 chasing one last major to complete the career Grand Slam — while his golden-boy son (Jimmy Tatro) and foul-mouthed ex-wife (Molly Shannon) all know he's cooked. It's dumb-funny in the best Ferrell way, set to a hair-metal theme song. Perfect no-brain weekend binge.
items.say: Have you seen The Hawk on Netflix? Will Ferrell as a washed-up golf pro trying one last comeback — it's exactly as goofy as that sounds. Easy weekend watch. You a Ferrell guy?

links.text: Variety · Will Ferrell stars in Netflix golf comedy 'The Hawk'
